q: What is CCI?
a: CCI stands for the College of Computing and Informatics at the University of Sharjah. It is one of the leading technology colleges in the UAE, offering undergraduate and graduate programs in computing and information technology.

q: What does CCI stand for?
a: CCI stands for College of Computing and Informatics, part of the University of Sharjah.

q: Tell me about the College of Computing and Informatics
a: The College of Computing and Informatics at the University of Sharjah consists of three departments: Computer Science, Computer Engineering, and Information Systems. It offers multiple undergraduate and graduate programs accredited by ABET and the UAE Ministry of Education.

q: What departments does CCI have?
a: CCI has three departments: the Department of Computer Science, the Department of Computer Engineering, and the Department of Information Systems.

q: What programs does CCI offer?
a: CCI offers undergraduate programs in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, IT Multimedia, Business Information Systems, Biomedical Informatics, and Cybersecurity Engineering. At the graduate level, it offers an MSc in Data Science and other postgraduate programs.

q: What undergraduate programs are available?
a: CCI's undergraduate programs include: BSc in Computer Science, BSc in Computer Engineering, BSc in IT Multimedia, BSc in Business Information Systems, BSc in Biomedical Informatics, and BSc in Cybersecurity Engineering.

q: What is the Computer Science program?
a: The BSc in Computer Science is a 123 credit-hour program spread over eight semesters, completable in four years. It provides a solid foundation in computing with hands-on training in areas like artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and data analytics.

q: How many credit hours is the Computer Science degree?
a: The BSc in Computer Science requires 123 credit hours completed over eight semesters, approximately four years.

q: What is the Computer Engineering program?
a: The BSc in Computer Engineering requires 132 credit hours. Graduates can pursue careers as computer systems designers, digital system designers, IT security officers, embedded circuit designers, and software developers.

q: How many credit hours is the Computer Engineering degree?
a: The BSc in Computer Engineering requires 132 credit hours.

q: What is Business Information Systems?
a: The BSc in Business Information Systems (BIS) is offered by the Department of Information Systems. It combines computing with business knowledge, preparing students for careers at the intersection of technology and management.

q: What is IT Multimedia?
a: The BSc in IT Multimedia is an undergraduate program that blends information technology with multimedia design and digital media, preparing students for creative and technical roles in the digital industry.

q: What is Cybersecurity Engineering?
a: The BSc in Cybersecurity Engineering prepares students to protect digital systems, networks, and data from cyber threats. It covers topics such as cryptography, network security, and ethical hacking.

q: What is Biomedical Informatics?
a: The BSc in Biomedical Informatics combines computing and healthcare, training students to manage and analyze medical data and build health information systems.

q: Does CCI offer a Master's degree?
a: Yes, CCI offers graduate programs including the MSc in Data Science. Postgraduate study options are available through the college's departments.

q: What is the MSc in Data Science?
a: The MSc in Data Science is a graduate program that focuses on research and applications in computer science, statistics, and data analytics. It prepares students for advanced roles in data-driven industries.

q: Is CCI accredited?
a: Yes, CCI programs are accredited by ABET (Accreditation Board for Engineering and Technology) and the UAE Ministry of Education, ensuring international recognition and high academic standards.

q: What is ABET accreditation?
a: ABET is the Accreditation Board for Engineering and Technology, a prestigious international accreditation body. ABET accreditation of CCI programs confirms their quality and global recognition.

q: What labs does CCI have?
a: CCI has 13 IT labs distributed across five buildings on the university campus, equipped for both teaching and advanced research. The Computer Science department also has dedicated networking labs on both the men's and women's campuses.

q: How many labs does CCI have?
a: CCI has 13 IT labs across five university buildings, plus specialized labs for networking and research in the Computer Science department.

q: What research areas does CCI focus on?
a: CCI's faculty conduct research in areas including artificial intelligence, robotics, cybersecurity, data analytics, and computer networks. Faculty members have received prestigious awards and research grants both nationally and regionally.

q: What can I do with a Computer Science degree?
a: A BSc in Computer Science from CCI prepares you for careers in software development, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, data science, systems analysis, and more — in both the UAE and globally.

q: What jobs can I get with a Computer Engineering degree?
a: Computer Engineering graduates can work as computer systems or network designers, digital system designers, IT security officers, embedded circuit designers, and software or web developers.

q: How do I apply to UoS?
a: To apply, submit your application form and required documents to the Admissions Department by the stated deadline and pay the application fee of AED 360. Documents include a valid passport, secondary school certificate, residence visa (if applicable), a recent photo, and a health fitness certificate.

q: What are the admission requirements for UoS?
a: Applicants must have completed secondary education with the required average (obtained no more than three years prior), not have been expelled from any institution, and submit required documents including passport, school certificate, and health certificate, along with an AED 360 application fee.

q: What is the application fee for UoS?
a: The application fee for the University of Sharjah is AED 360.

q: What documents are required for admission?
a: Required documents include: a valid passport copy, secondary school certificate, UAE National Family Registry (for UAE nationals) or residence visa (for non-nationals), a recent passport-size photo, and a health fitness certificate.

q: What is the CCI event?
a: The CCI Event is an annual showcase where students from the College of Computing and Informatics demonstrate their projects and innovations. It is a great opportunity to see real student work in areas like AI, software, cybersecurity, and more.

q: What is happening at this event?
a: This is the CCI Event, where students from the College of Computing and Informatics at the University of Sharjah present their final-year and research projects. You can explore student innovations across computing and technology.

q: Who is presenting at the CCI event?
a: Students from the College of Computing and Informatics are presenting their projects. They come from programs including Computer Science, Computer Engineering, IT Multimedia, Cybersecurity, and more.

q: What kind of projects are students showing?
a: Students are showcasing projects across various computing fields, including artificial intelligence, mobile and web applications, cybersecurity systems, robotics, data analytics, and biomedical informatics.

q: Can I join CCI?
a: Yes! If you are interested in joining CCI, visit the University of Sharjah Admissions Department or the official website at sharjah.ac.ae to learn about requirements and deadlines.

q: Where is CCI located?
a: The College of Computing and Informatics is located on the University of Sharjah campus in Sharjah, UAE. The university has separate men's and women's campuses, and CCI has labs in multiple buildings across the campus.

q: Where is the University of Sharjah?
a: The University of Sharjah is located in Sharjah, United Arab Emirates. It is one of the leading universities in the region.

q: How can I contact CCI?
a: You can contact the University of Sharjah through the official website at sharjah.ac.ae. The admissions office is available Monday through Thursday, from 8:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.

q: What are the working hours of the university?
a: University offices are generally available Monday through Thursday, from 8:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.

q: How long does it take to complete a degree at CCI?
a: Most undergraduate programs at CCI take four years to complete. The BSc in Computer Science is 8 semesters over four years. Program lengths may vary slightly by degree.

q: What is the University of Sharjah?
a: The University of Sharjah is a leading comprehensive university in the UAE, founded by His Highness Sheikh Dr. Sultan bin Muhammad Al Qasimi. It offers programs across engineering, science, medicine, arts, and more.

q: Is CCI good?
a: CCI is one of the top computing colleges in the UAE. Its programs are ABET accredited, faculty actively publish in high-impact journals, and students are equipped with skills in AI, cybersecurity, robotics, and data science.

q: What makes CCI special?
a: CCI stands out for its ABET-accredited programs, state-of-the-art labs, active research culture, and diverse program offerings spanning computer science, engineering, cybersecurity, AI, and biomedical informatics — all within the University of Sharjah.
